commands.review_summary:
The PR reclassifies title-only Readability output as incomplete, tries configured provider fallback before body-scoped raw HTML cleanup, and adds focused web_fetch regression tests.

PR surface: Source +212, Tests +233. Total +445 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the title-only Readability shape: current-main source accepts any non-empty Readability text before fallback, and the PR body supplies copied live output showing that shape falling through to raw-html after the fix. The exact reporter URL or HTML fixture is still unavailable.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the provider-first compatibility and cost tradeoff; no narrow mechanical repair is identified.

target.title: fix(web-fetch): extract full HTML body content
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #82685 by treating title-only Readability output as failed body extraction instead of returning only the page title.
- Preserves the configured-provider-before-raw-HTML order for title-only Readability pages, so existing provider routing remains the first recovery path when configured.
- Hardens the runtime body scanner so fake `<body>` tags inside comments, raw-text containers, and inert/nested `<template>` regions do not replace the real visible document body.
- Keeps the change scoped to the `web_fetch` runtime extraction path; shared HTML-to-markdown helpers, provider configuration, network policy, and public API shape are unchanged.

What problem does this PR solve?

`web_fetch` could accept Readability output that only contained the page title as successful body extraction. In that state, users and agents could receive only the title instead of visible page content. The runtime body scanner also needed to preserve the document-body boundary while skipping HTML regions that can legally contain literal fake body markup but are not the visible body.

Why does this matter now?

The linked issue is user-visible: downstream tool output is misleading when `web_fetch` returns title-only text or hidden/inert fake body content instead of the actual page body.

What is the intended outcome?

This is a root-cause extraction-state fix: title-only Readability output no longer completes extraction, and body-boundary scanning now enforces the browser-visible body invariant before raw HTML text is produced. Successful behavior is provider output when a configured provider handles the title-only page, or visible body text from local raw HTML cleanup when no provider output is available.

What is intentionally out of scope?

This PR does not change shared `htmlToMarkdown`, provider configuration, network/auth/security policy, migrations, schema, public API shape, or the provider catalog. It only changes the `web_fetch` runtime extraction decision and focused regression coverage for that path.

What should reviewers focus on?

Please review the source-of-truth boundary between local Readability extraction, configured provider routing, and runtime-local raw HTML body cleanup. The provider compatibility boundary is preserved: title-only pages still try configured provider routing before local raw HTML cleanup, while scanner hardening only affects the local cleanup path when it is reached.

## Linked context

- Closes #82685.
- Updates PR #89028 after review feedback called out missing real-behavior proof, raw-text/template scanner gaps, and provider compatibility risk.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `web_fetch` no longer returns only the page title when Readability emits title-only text; it continues extraction and returns visible body content when no configured provider output is available.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw `web_fetch` tool execution with the real Readability extractor and a deterministic HTML fetch fixture. External network access was not required for this proof.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran a Node proof script that calls the real Readability extractor, then calls `createWebFetchTool().execute` with `{ extractMode: 'markdown' }` against the same title-only fixture with provider fetching disabled.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  ```text
  web_fetch title-only fallback proof
  fixture url: https://fixture.openclaw.test/title-only-template-raw-text
  readability.title: "Sample Page"
  readability.text: "Sample Page"
  readability.titleOnly: true
  web_fetch.status: 200
  web_fetch.contentType: text/html
  web_fetch.extractor: raw-html
  web_fetch.title.hasSamplePage: true
  web_fetch.text.hasBodyMarker: true
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the fixture forced title-only Readability output, and with no configured provider output available, the `web_fetch` result used `raw-html` with the expected body marker present.
- What was not tested: the exact original reporter page, because the issue did not include a concrete URL or full HTML fixture.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the live proof uses a deterministic fixture so the title-only condition and expected body marker are reproducible. The configured-provider order is covered by a focused regression test that asserts provider output wins before local raw HTML cleanup when a provider is configured.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the new focused regressions failed before this fix: the nested-template raw-text case surfaced fake script body text, and the provider-order guard caught the intermediate regression where local raw HTML cleanup ran before the configured provider.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts -t "ignores fake body tags in raw-text content inside inert templates"` — passed after the nested-template/raw-text scanner fix.
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts -t "tries provider fallback before body HTML when readability returns only the page title"` — passed after restoring provider-first order; configured provider output wins before local raw HTML cleanup.
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts` — passed, 62 tests.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added coverage for fake body tags inside raw-text content nested within inert templates, proving the scanner returns the real body marker and excludes the fake body text.
- Restored title-only/provider coverage so configured provider output is used before local raw HTML cleanup when a provider is configured.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The nested-template raw-text regression initially returned fake script body text instead of the real body marker.
- The provider-order guard caught an intermediate regression where recoverable local body HTML bypassed the configured provider.

If no test was added, why not?

- Not applicable; focused regression coverage was added/updated for the scanner gap and provider compatibility boundary.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. For title-only Readability pages, `web_fetch` now continues extraction instead of stopping at the title. Configured provider order is preserved, and local raw HTML body cleanup is used only when provider output is unavailable.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No expansion of capability. The change stays inside `web_fetch` extraction and does not broaden network access, auth, secrets, or tool execution.

What is the highest-risk area?

Compatibility for title-only pages: `web_fetch` now treats title-only Readability as incomplete and may proceed to configured provider routing or local raw HTML cleanup instead of returning only the title.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The canonical source of truth remains `web_fetch` runtime extraction: Readability first, configured provider routing second, local raw HTML cleanup last for HTML extraction failures.
- The change is limited to title-only Readability output; normal successful Readability output is unchanged.
- The focused provider regression asserts that configured provider output still wins before local raw HTML cleanup.
- Raw HTML cleanup stays runtime-local to `web_fetch`; shared conversion helpers and public API shape are unchanged.
- Related open PR scan result: PR #89028 is the active repair path for #82685, and current main did not already contain this title-only/body-boundary fix when this update started.
- Patch-quality note: the added `null`/`undefined` parser results are explicit scanner miss signals for malformed/non-tag input, not symptom masking; they allow scanning to continue until the real body boundary is found or no body is present.
- Size note: the diff is confined to one runtime file and its focused tests; the line count is mostly regression coverage for existing review findings in this PR.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review after the updated branch is submitted and CI settles.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Maintainer: review the title-only extraction behavior with provider-first compatibility preserved.
- CI: rerun on the updated PR branch after submission.
- External proof: no exact reporter URL/HTML was available, so proof uses representative deterministic HTML that reproduces the title-only/body-cleanup invariant.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001/RF-008 fixed: raw-text content inside inert/nested templates is skipped while scanning body boundaries.
- RF-002/RF-004/RF-006 fixed: PR body now includes copied real `web_fetch` tool output for the title-only/body-cleanup behavior and describes the linked issue limitation.
- RF-003/RF-005 fixed: provider-first order is preserved for configured providers, addressing ClawSweeper's regression finding about local raw HTML cleanup bypassing configured provider routing.
- RF-007 fixed: author-side proof and regression-test gaps are addressed in this update.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
